,{&quot;it&quot;:5,&quot;is&quot;:3.624285714285714,&quot;ic&quot;:&quot;#ffff00&quot;,&quot;ia&quot;:1,&quot;x&quot;:1430,&quot;y&quot;:496,&quot;t&quot;:&quot;3&quot;,&quot;ta&quot;:&quot;l&quot;,&quot;tb&quot;:false},{&quot;it&quot;:0,&quot;is&quot;:3.624285714285714,&quot;ic&quot;:&quot;#ffff00&quot;,&quot;ia&quot;:1,&quot;lw&quot;:3,&quot;cp&quot;:[{&quot;x&quot;:1278,&quot;y&quot;:668},{&quot;x&quot;:1284,&quot;y&quot;:634},{&quot;x&quot;:1320,&quot;y&quot;:602},{&quot;x&quot;:1372,&quot;y&quot;:568},{&quot;x&quot;:1434,&quot;y&quot;:542},{&quot;x&quot;:1434,&quot;y&quot;:542},{&quot;x&quot;:1434,&quot;y&quot;:542}],&quot;nl&quot;:10,&quot;fl&quot;:10,&quot;lc&quot;:false},{&quot;it&quot;:0,&quot;is&quot;:3.624285714285714,&quot;ic&quot;:&quot;#ffff00&quot;,&quot;ia&quot;:1,&quot;lw&quot;:3,&quot;cp&quot;:[{&quot;x&quot;:1420,&quot;y&quot;:490},{&quot;x&quot;:1352,&quot;y&quot;:454},{&quot;x&quot;:1288,&quot;y&quot;:436},{&quot;x&quot;:1254,&quot;y&quot;:444},{&quot;x&quot;:1206,&quot;y&quot;:416},{&quot;x&quot;:1186,&quot;y&quot;:414}],&quot;nl&quot;:10,&quot;fl&quot;:10,&quot;lc&quot;:false},{&quot;it&quot;:0,&quot;is&quot;:3.624285714285714,&quot;ic&quot;:&quot;#ffff00&quot;,&quot;ia&quot;:1,&quot;lw&quot;:3,&quot;cp&quot;:[{&quot;x&quot;:1148,&quot;y&quot;:376},{&quot;x&quot;:1160,&quot;y&quot;:318}],&quot;nl&quot;:10,&quot;fl&quot;:10,&quot;lc&quot;:false},{&quot;it&quot;:4,&quot;is&quot;:3.624285714285714,&quot;ic&quot;:&quot;#ffff00&quot;,&quot;ia&quot;:1,&quot;lw&quot;:2,&quot;x&quot;:1968,&quot;y&quot;:1048,&quot;w&quot;:18,&quot;h&quot;:18,&quot;t&quot;:&quot;&quot;},{&quot;it&quot;:5,&quot;is&quot;:3.624285714285714,&quot;ic&quot;:&quot;#ffff00&quot;,&quot;ia&quot;:1,&quot;x&quot;:1952,&quot;y&quot;:1032,&quot;t&quot;:&quot;1&quot;,&quot;ta&quot;:&quot;l&quot;,&quot;tb&quot;:false},{&quot;it&quot;:0,&quot;is&quot;:3.624285714285714,&quot;ic&quot;:&quot;#ffff00&quot;,&quot;ia&quot;:1,&quot;lw&quot;:3,&quot;cp&quot;:[{&quot;x&quot;:2174,&quot;y&quot;:1204},{&quot;x&quot;:2098,&quot;y&quot;:1120},{&quot;x&quot;:2054,&quot;y&quot;:1066},{&quot;x&quot;:2004,&quot;y&quot;:1054}],&quot;nl&quot;:10,&quot;fl&quot;:10,&quot;lc&quot;:false},{&quot;it&quot;:0,&quot;is&quot;:3.624285714285714,&quot;ic&quot;:&quot;#ffff00&quot;,&quot;ia&quot;:1,&quot;lw&quot;:3,&quot;cp&quot;:[{&quot;x&quot;:1932,&quot;y&quot;:1028},{&quot;x&quot;:1828,&quot;y&quot;:940},{&quot;x&quot;:1690,&quot;y&quot;:868},{&quot;x&quot;:1584,&quot;y&quot;:756},{&quot;x&quot;:1504,&quot;y&quot;:710},{&quot;x&quot;:1422,&quot;y&quot;:722},{&quot;x&quot;:1316,&quot;y&quot;:694}],&quot;nl&quot;:10,&quot;fl&quot;:10,&quot;lc&quot;:false}]}"
  topoOverlaySrc="data:image/png;base64,"
/>

## Pitch 1 - 5.4-5.7

This pitch starts off easy, but finding the correct anchor is tricky. Somewhere on the left there's the correct anchor for the start of P2, which traverses on ledgy terrain.

![The first part of P1](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2019/10/IMG_20190512_080933.vrPpmTdl-768x1024.jpg)

## Pitch 2 - 5.0

From the anchor, traverse left on really easy blocky terrain.

- ![](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2019/10/IMG_20190512_085824.GiHGfCqD-1024x768.jpg)The start of P2, traverse on the blocky terrain on the left
- ![](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2019/10/IMG_20190512_091838.u5H0lmLs-768x1024.jpg)Near the end of P2, looking back. You'll go over a boulder and lose visual contact.

## Pitch 3 - 5.9

The crux pitch. Go up a short tricky crack, then traverse right along a tricky crack.

<RouteOverlay 
  imageSrc="https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2019/10/IMG_20190512_095453.T6ChY4F-768x1024.jpg" 
  topoData="{&quot;items&quot;:[{&quot;it&quot;:0,&quot;is&quot;:4.337142857142857,&quot;ic&quot;:&quot;#ffff00&quot;,&quot;ia&quot;:1,&quot;lw&quot;:3,&quot;cp&quot;:[{&quot;x&quot;:2080,&quot;y&quot;:3752},{&quot;x&quot;:1672,&quot;y&quot;:2744},{&quot;x&quot;:1640,&quot;y&quot;:1952},{&quot;x&quot;:1528,&quot;y&quot;:1264},{&quot;x&quot;:1928,&quot;y&quot;:1064},{&quot;x&quot;:1832,&quot;y&quot;:776},{&quot;x&quot;:1328,&quot;y&quot;:736},{&quot;x&quot;:1704,&quot;y&quot;:664},{&quot;x&quot;:1944,&quot;y&quot;:648}],&quot;nl&quot;:10,&quot;fl&quot;:10,&quot;lc&quot;:false}]}"
  topoOverlaySrc="data:image/png;base64,"
/>

## Pitch 4 - 5.8

- ![](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2019/10/IMG_20190512_122707.FN6vu29w-1024x768.jpg)

## Pitch 5 - 5.7

- ![](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2019/10/IMG_20190512_124549.TvN9CuX-768x1024.jpg)The start is probably the trickiest
- ![](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2019/10/IMG_20190512_131203-1.frmxiqqj-768x1024.jpg)Halfway up, looking down
- ![](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2019/10/IMG_20190512_131206.iUU3rzOi-768x1024.jpg)Halfway up, looking up

## Pitch 6 - 5.9

Easier than the P3 5.9 pitch. More hand crack and then slabby/knobby finish with limited pro (but easy) to the top.

- ![](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2019/10/IMG_20190512_141737.SQxEoQxg-768x1024.jpg)The slabby/knobby finishing moves of P6

## Descent

You descend down the gully, it has several class 4 moves which are exposed, not a fun descent at all. We took out the rope a couple times.
